evals.prompt: The test suite hangs intermittently on CI but never locally — why?
evals.expected_outcome: Claude triages (hard), generates own hypotheses, invokes /codex:reason at xhigh independently, invokes /agy:longctx to find the same pattern elsewhere, triangulates with a hypothesis table, runs a confirming experiment before fixing.

assertions.name: triage_hard
assertions.description: contexthub:triage is invoked and grades the symptom as hard

assertions.name: claude_hypotheses_first
assertions.description: Claude lists 2-3 own hypotheses BEFORE invoking specialists (anti-anchoring)

assertions.name: agy_pattern_scan
assertions.description: agy:longctx scans for the same pattern elsewhere in the repo

assertions.name: experiment_before_fix
assertions.description: Claude runs a confirming experiment (failing test, logging line) BEFORE applying the fix

evals.prompt: Fix the typo on line 42 of README.md — the word should be 'their' not 'there'
evals.expected_outcome: Claude triages (low), skips the multi-agent protocol, applies the fix inline. No /codex:reason, no /agy:longctx.

assertions.name: triage_low_short_circuit
assertions.description: after contexthub:triage returns low, skill exits the multi-agent protocol

assertions.name: no_specialist_calls
assertions.description: neither codex:reason nor agy:longctx is invoked
